name: feature-probe
$comment: Phase 6 wire shape: _action carries {name} only. Parameters that used to ride in `context` (redirect URL, storage values, download URL/filename, form note, sort column/direction, table filter, page number) now live in dedicated state slots on FeatureProbeState (Plan 06-04). The fixture writes them via stateMutations to mimic the renderer's bind writes that would happen before each user click. Table sort uses per-column action names (table-sort-name / table-sort-status); pagination uses prevAction/nextAction (table-page-prev / table-page-next). 1.11.0: buildVm now also renders a layout:"row" section (with LinkNode children); it is static view-shape captured by every GET step (initial/fresh-*), so the byte-identical diff across .NET/bun/node covers the new SectionNode field without a dedicated action step. 1.12.0: buildVm additionally renders the arrange/align alignment vocabulary as static view-shape — a bare layout:"row" section with NEITHER arrange nor align (proving omitted = absent on the wire), the canonical header-bar (row + arrange:"space-between" + a heading TextNode first child + a nested row nav cluster, ALIGN-04), one row per remaining arrange value (start/center/end/space-around/space-evenly), and one row per align value (start/center/end/stretch/baseline). All captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers PageNode/SectionNode.arrange + .align with no new step. 1.13.0: buildVm additionally renders the switcher vocabulary as static view-shape — a bare layout:"switcher" section with NEITHER threshold nor limit (proving omitted = absent on the wire), one switcher per threshold value (sm/md/lg/xl), and a switcher with limit:4 set (6 LinkNode children). All captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers PageNode/SectionNode.layout:"switcher" + .threshold + .limit with no new step. 1.13.0 also: buildVm renders the cards minItem vocabulary as static view-shape — a SECTION-level bare layout:"cards" section with NO minItem (proving omitted = absent on the wire, even though the page root is already layout:"cards"), and one layout:"cards" section per minItem value (xs/sm/md/lg/xl). All captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers PageNode/SectionNode.minItem with no new step. Phase 10 (fits node): buildVm additionally renders the new fits node as static view-shape — a fits with `axis` OMITTED (proving omitted = absent on the wire) and a fits with `axis:"both"` (proving the axis field present as the JSON string "both"), each wrapped in a labeled section and carrying preferred-first/fallback-last candidate children (a layout:"row" candidate first, a layout:"stack" candidate last). All captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers the {type:"fits", axis?, children} wire with no new step. NOTE: the fits node's CLIENT-SIDE measure-and-pick selection is browser-only (ResizeObserver + DOM layout in BrowserAdapter) and is NOT part of parity — parity proves only that the node serializes identically across backends. 2.1.0 (LinkNode.active): the canonical header-bar nav cluster marks its first link active:true ("you are here") in both backends; the remaining links omit active (proving omitted = absent on the wire, matching the .NET bool?+WhenWritingNull posture). Captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers LinkNode.active with no new step. 3.0.0 (unified appearance axes): buildVm renders an "Appearance axes" section as static view-shape covering the new vocabulary — button emphasis/tone/size (+ a primary×danger×lg combo and a copy-button with all three), section tone (a card+tone and a bare tone), text tone (+ heading×tone), list-item state+tone, and table-row state+tone. The old fused `variant` is gone (buttons→emphasis/tone, list/row→state/tone, text style→tone). Captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers every new field with no new step. 3.2.0 (child-side modifiers): buildVm renders a "Child modifiers (alignSelf + maxWidth)" section as static view-shape — a bare variant:"card" child (NEITHER alignSelf nor maxWidth => proves omitted = absent on the wire), one card per alignSelf value (start/center/end), one card per maxWidth value (half/two-thirds/three-quarters/prose), and a two-bubble chat composition (alignSelf + maxWidth + tone). Captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers SectionNode.alignSelf + .maxWidth with no new step. Feedback primitives: buildVm renders a 'Feedback primitives' section as static view-shape — three BadgeNodes (bare => tone/emphasis absent, tone-only, tone+emphasis) and two EmptyStateNodes (bare => message/action absent, and one with message + a CTA ButtonNode whose unique action name feedback-cta is reached by the action-name walk descending into empty-state.action). Captured by the existing GET steps. The new trigger-toast POST step returns TWO toast side-effects (a bare message-only toast and a full tone+durationMs toast), byte-diffing the {type:'toast', message, tone?, durationMs?} side-effect wire across all backends (the toast's client-side display is browser-only and NOT part of parity).
